title: artemisia campestris var. scouleriana
intro: Artemisia campestris var. scouleriana, also known as Scouler's sagebrush or western mugwort, is a variety of the wormwood plant that is native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Scouler's sagebrush include relieving digestive issues, reducing pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. Scouler's sagebrush can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional folk medicine and by herbalists to support overall health and well-being.
